plan_steps.description: Provide programming assistance by explaining the error and suggesting solutions. The error 'SyntaxError: Unexpected token '<' at line 1, column 1' typically indicates that the parser encountered an HTML tag or an unexpected character at the beginning of the file, which is not valid in most programming languages. This often happens when a file is mistakenly treated as code when it's actually HTML or vice-versa, or if there's a misconfiguration in the build/compilation process.

parameters.description: Explain the 'SyntaxError: Unexpected token '<' at line 1, column 1. Compilation failed.' error. Advise Pratik Sir to check the beginning of the code file for HTML tags or unexpected characters. Suggest verifying the file type and the project's build configuration. Recommend using the code editor's error checking and seeking help if needed.
